jokes.text: Who else is gonna tell her that he's become the Three Faces of Weird?
jokes.setup: Louie refers to Latka's multiple personality disorder
jokes.punchline: Crude reference to 'Three Faces of Eve' calling it 'Three Faces of Weird'
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insensitivity with a clever pop culture twist on 'Three Faces of Eve'. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I was talking about the gentleman who suffers from...
jokes.setup: Louie attempts to be more sensitive after being called out
jokes.punchline: Trails off, unable to actually be more sensitive
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's inability to maintain even fake sensitivity. Good character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Eh, twice on my face and once on my back.
jokes.setup: Someone asks how many times Latka has shaved today
jokes.punchline: He's shaved his back once - unexpected oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and unexpected detail. Classic Latka oversharing in his innocent way.

jokes.text: I don't even like American women. I have a good reason, too. What's that? They don't give me the time of day.
jokes.setup: Latka claims he doesn't like American women and has a good reason
jokes.punchline: The reason is they reject him - self-defeating logic
jokes.explanation: Good structure with unexpected self-awareness. Shows Latka's innocent honesty about his romantic failures.

jokes.text: Why are you carrying a styptic pencil in your ear? Well, in light of what just happened, a better question is, 'Why aren't you?'
jokes.setup: Reasonable question about Jim's odd behavior
jokes.punchline: Jim turns it around with impeccable logic given the situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim logic - turns the absurd into the reasonable. Great structure and highly quotable with perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Elaine, you know what it feels like to be used, taken advantage of and then thrown away like an old shoe? Does she.
jokes.setup: Simka asks Elaine about being used and discarded
jokes.punchline: Louie's brutal confirmation about Elaine's romantic history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie cruelty with excellent timing. The economy is superb - just two words that devastate.

jokes.text: Simka. Latka. Simka.
jokes.setup: Romantic reunion building tension
jokes.punchline: They just keep saying each other's names awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing their awkwardness. Simple but effective character work.

jokes.text: I feel very comfortable in that tongue, you bet, Joe!
jokes.setup: Latka commenting on speaking English
jokes.punchline: Adds random American slang 'you bet, Joe!' inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka character work - mixing formal speech with random American slang inappropriately.

jokes.text: Whew! This is just like in Body Heat.
jokes.setup: Watching Latka and Simka's awkward formal reunion
jokes.punchline: Compares it to the steamy movie Body Heat - completely inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between what we're seeing and the reference. Economy works well but fairly predictable pop culture joke.

jokes.text: Yes, he's kookiputz.
jokes.setup: Alex explains Latka has multiple personality disorder
jokes.punchline: Simka uses made-up foreign word that sounds like clinical term but dismissive
jokes.explanation: Excellent made-up word that sounds both foreign and dismissive. Perfect economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: Eh, would you like something from the hoof or something close to the tusk?
jokes.setup: Latka serving warthog dinner to Simka
jokes.punchline: Offers cuts of meat by anatomical reference in grossly descriptive way
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist food description that's both disgusting and charming. Perfect character moment for Latka's foreign customs.

jokes.text: Both! That's what I love about you, you eat like a swine.
jokes.setup: Simka enthusiastically accepts both gross meat options
jokes.punchline: Latka says he loves that she 'eats like a swine' as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect cultural comedy - what sounds like an insult is clearly a compliment in their culture. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Well, you know, it hasn't been all brethnees and warthog for Mama Gravas's little boy either.
jokes.setup: Simka mentions lonely nights
jokes.punchline: Latka references the dinner items and his mother in overly poetic way
jokes.explanation: Great callback that elevates mundane dinner items to poetic metaphor. Perfect Latka voice and character integration.

jokes.text: What died in here? Oh, my gosh! Where am I? The Ramada Inn of Rumania?
jokes.setup: Vic personality emerges and smells the warthog dinner
jokes.punchline: Makes crude joke about death smell then specific Ramada Inn of Rumania crack
jokes.explanation: Good personality switch comedy. Vic's sleazy character contrasts perfectly with Latka's romantic setup.

jokes.text: Well, uh, that's your decision, but one thing. Leave and I'll die.
jokes.setup: Simka says she should leave
jokes.punchline: Vic's melodramatic threat delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between casual delivery and dramatic content. Perfect Vic sleazy charm.

jokes.text: You were a daddy's girl. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Vic claims to read Simka's personality
jokes.punchline: Generic psychology that could apply to anyone
jokes.explanation: Shows Vic's fake insight with generic pickup line psychology. Solid character work but predictable.

jokes.text: you have my name tattooed on your right thigh. Oh, I don't! Hey, okay, the evening's young.
jokes.setup: Vic claims she has his name tattooed on her thigh
jokes.punchline: When she denies it, he suggests the night is young
jokes.explanation: Perfect sleazy Vic line with good setup-punchline structure. The confidence is what makes it funny.

jokes.text: Well, in my country, there is only one thing a girl can say to that, which is one of the reasons I left.
jokes.setup: Vic says he wants to love her
jokes.punchline: Simka implies her country's response would be restrictive/negative
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Simka's liberation from conservative culture. Great structure and earned humor.

jokes.text: Well, for instance, you can say, that you were up all night with him, and you discussed, uh, whether the Today show is better with Bryant Gumbel. I cannot say that.
jokes.setup: Latka hopes they discussed something innocent like TV
jokes.punchline: Simka can't lie - they obviously did more than talk
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with specific topical reference. Shows Latka's desperate hope and Simka's honesty.

jokes.text: Did I whimper? Was I... clumsy? No, you were wonderful! Then it wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Latka asks about his performance
jokes.punchline: When told he was wonderful, concludes it couldn't have been him
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-deprecating humor with excellent logic and structure. Highly quotable and great character insight.

jokes.text: You have shamed me, degraded yourself, disgraced your family, cheapened your people, and blemished your planet.
jokes.setup: Latka listing Simka's transgressions
jokes.punchline: Escalates from personal to planetary scale
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to planetary scope. Perfect Latka melodrama and very quotable.

jokes.text: I hate you and I never want to see you again, and thanks for a wonderful night!
jokes.setup: Simka dramatically storming out angry at Latka
jokes.punchline: Adds sincere thanks for the night with Vic
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction showing the absurdity of the situation. Excellent economy and structure with high quotability.

jokes.text: Oh... I can't tell you how many times I've had something like that happen. Talk to me. Okay. She says the other man was Vic. Talk to Alex.
jokes.setup: Tony offers to help with relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs out when he learns it's multiple personality related
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Tony's quick retreat from complexity. Great economy in the reversal.

jokes.text: Vic has more fun in his pockets than I have in my whole life.
jokes.setup: Latka going through Vic's belongings
jokes.punchline: Compares Vic's pocket contents to his entire life experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating line with perfect economy. Great character insight and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Where are they? I don't know. I've asked all of them.
jokes.setup: Alex says there are women who prefer innocent qualities
jokes.punchline: Latka says he's already asked all of them
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with excellent economy. Implies both small number and his thoroughness.

jokes.text: You're gonna just sit there and mope? No, I'm going to whine and snivel.
jokes.setup: Alex asks if Latka will just mope
jokes.punchline: Latka corrects him with worse activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Latka's honest self-awareness. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I am responsible. Yes, I-I have to save her from that two-bit bossa nova.
jokes.setup: Latka realizes he's responsible for the situation
jokes.punchline: Calls Vic a 'two-bit bossa nova' - mixing insult with music reference
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that captures Vic's sleazy lounge lizard persona perfectly. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: And if he won't listen to good manners, I'm going to have to get rough. I would hate to be in his shoes.
jokes.setup: Latka threatens to get rough with Vic
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically says he'd hate to be in Vic's shoes (knowing Latka isn't threatening)
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony with Alex's sarcastic support. Shows the absurdity of Latka threatening himself.

jokes.text: Men have gone to almost certain injury in the cabs.
jokes.setup: Latka explaining how lovesick he is affects his work
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement about taxi passenger safety
jokes.explanation: Good Latka melodrama with workplace consequences. Character-appropriate exaggeration.

jokes.text: Oh, come on now, why would I want something like that?
jokes.setup: Simka accuses Vic of only wanting physical pleasure
jokes.punchline: Vic's obviously disingenuous denial
jokes.explanation: Standard sleazy character denial but well-executed for Vic's personality. Predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: then you're hitting nail with your head, buddy boy!
jokes.setup: Simka wants Vic to go away
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'hitting nail on the head' with 'buddy boy'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism showing Simka's English skills while being assertive. Character-appropriate language error.

jokes.text: No tears, no bum feelings, no blame, no fault insurance.
jokes.setup: Vic giving his goodbye speech
jokes.punchline: Mixes breakup clichés with insurance terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative mixing of emotional language with business terms. Good Vic character moment with his sleazy charm.

jokes.text: And if you ever come near my woman again, I will call you a name!
jokes.setup: Latka threatening Vic
jokes.punchline: Ultimate threat is name-calling - very mild
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka character moment - thinks name-calling is a serious threat. Excellent structure and highly quotable. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: and no, I'm not going to beating on the bush.
jokes.setup: Latka trying to be direct
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'beating around the bush' with 'beating on the bush'
jokes.explanation: Standard Latka malapropism but well-executed and character-appropriate for his English skills.
